title: Lyme Disease Tests: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
hat flows in and around your brain and spinal cord. The test checks your sample for antibodies that your immune system Â makes to fight the bacteria that cause Lyme disease.
You can get Lyme disease if an infected tick bites you. Ticks can bite you anywhere on your body, but they usually bite in hard-to-see places such as your groin, scalp, backs of the knees, and armpits.
Most Lyme disease is caused by bites from black-legged ticks (also known as deer ticks), which are about the size of a sesame seed. Baby black-legged ticks are about the size of a poppy seed, so you may not know you have been bitten. In general, an infected tick needs to be attached to you for 36 to 48 hours before it can pass Lyme disease bacteria onto you.
Without treatment, Lyme disease can cause serious health problems that may affect your joints, heart, and nervous system . But if you find Lyme disease early, it can usually be cured after a few weeks of treatment with antibiotic Â medicine. If it's found later, treatment may take up to eight weeks.
Other names: Lyme antibodies detection, Borrelia burgdorferi antibodies test, Borrelia DNA Detection, IgM/IgG by Western Blot, Lyme disease test (CSF), Borrelia antibodies, IgM/IgG
What are they used for? 
Lyme disease tests are used to find out if you have a Lyme disease infection. Your health care provider is most likely to order the test if you have symptom
